title: 10 best herbal capsules for dry eyes

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like kaempferol and quercetin, which can help reduce inflammation in the eyes and alleviate symptoms of dry eye disease.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, contributing to the development of dry eye.

Immune system modulation: Kansas coneflower has been shown to modulate the immune response and reduce inflammation, which may help regulate the immune-mediated processes that contribute to dry eye disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with dry eyes because they contain Echinacea, a natural herb that has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties of Echinacea help reduce redness and itchiness associated with dry eye syndrome, while its antioxidant capabilities protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. By taking these capsules regularly, individuals can experience relief from the discomfort and irritation of dry eyes, promoting healthy vision and overall ocular well-being.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds may help alleviate dry eyes by reducing inflammation in the eyes and promoting the health of the tear-producing glands.

Phenolic Acids (specifically, kaempferol and quercetin): Phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help soothe and protect the eyes, promoting the health of the tear film and reducing dryness.

Alkaloids (specifically, echinacoside): Echinacoside has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce eye inflammation and promote the health of the ocular surface, potentially alleviating dry eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make capsules for dry eyes due to their high concentration of alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the eyes.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules for dry eyes because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate dry eye symptoms.

Flowers: They are used to make capsules for dry eyes as they contain glycosides, alkylamides, and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia roots from a reliable supplier or cultivate your own.
Clean the dried roots by rinsing with distilled water to remove any impurities or debris.
Grind the cleaned roots into a fine powder using a food processor or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground root powder with a binding agent such as gelatin or pullulan in a 1:1 ratio.
Fill empty capsule shells with the mixture and seal them according to the manufacturer's instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with dry eyes because they contain a rich source of antioxidants, flavonoids, and terpenoids that work synergistically to soothe and moisturize the eyes. The capsules' ability to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the eyes helps to alleviate dryness and irritation. Additionally, the herbal extracts in Maidenhair tree capsules have been shown to stabilize the tear film, allowing for more comfortable vision and reduced eye fatigue.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Ginkgo biloba contains antioxidants that help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with dry eye syndrome.

Vasodilatory Effects: The flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba capsules can dilate blood vessels and improve circulation to the eyes, increasing oxygen and nutrient delivery to the cornea and conjunctiva, which may help alleviate symptoms of dry eye disease.

Anti-inflammatory Effects: Ginkgo biloba has anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, which is a common symptom of dry eye syndrome, and also help to soothe and calm the affected area.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This triterpene helps with dry eyes by reducing inflammation and improving the integrity of the ocular surface, thus alleviating dryness and irritation.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin and Kaempferol): These phenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.

Ginkgolides (specifically Ginkgolide B): This diterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help improve blood flow to the eyes, reduce inflammation, and alleviate dryness, thereby relieving dry eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary part used in Ginkgo biloba capsules for dry eyes due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Seeds: The seeds are used as a secondary part in Ginkgo biloba capsules for dry eyes because they contain ginkgolides, which have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the eyes.
Fruits: The fruits are also used in some Ginkgo biloba capsules for dry eyes due to their high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help alleviate dry eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in the fall season when the leaves turn yellow and fall from the tree.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves in a single layer at 70-80 degrees fahrenheit for 3-5 days.
Grind 1,000-2,000 mg of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 500-1,000 mg of the ginkgo biloba powder with 100-200 mg of a natural capsule filler and 50-100 mg of an anti-caking agent.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the ginkgo biloba mixture and seal them using a capsule machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi capsules helps with dry eyes because they contain the herb Bacopa monnieri, which has been traditionally used to improve memory and cognitive function. However, research suggests that it also possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and moisturize dry, irritated eyes. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy eye function, brahmi capsules may provide natural relief from dry eye symptoms such as itchiness, redness, and blurred vision. This makes them a popular natural remedy for people suffering from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Cognitive Enhancing Properties: Brahmi capsules contain compounds that enhance cognitive function, which can help to improve focus and reduce eye fatigue caused by prolonged screen time or reading, reducing symptoms of dry eyes.
Antioxidant Activity: Brahmi has antioxidant properties that help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and irritation in the eyes.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Brahmi capsules may help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, alleviating dry eye symptoms such as redness, itchiness, and burning sensations.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides, a type of saponin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, alleviating dry eye symptoms.

Bitter Alkaloids: Bitter alkaloids in Bacopa monnieri, such as brahmine, have been shown to improve the aqueous tear production and enhance the mucin secretion in the eyes, which can help maintain tear film stability and reduce dryness.

Phenolic Compounds: Phenolic compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have antioxidant properties that can protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye syndrome.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of active compounds, including bacosides, which are believed to provide relief from dry eyes.
Roots: The roots of Bacopa monnieri contain a significant amount of bacosides, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and protect dry, irritated eyes.
Stems: The stems are also used, as they contain a combination of bacosides and other bioactive compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy tear production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of bacopa monnieri leaves and stems from mature plants in the morning.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at 40°c for 24 to 48 hours.
Weigh out 30 grams of dried bacopa monnieri and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 30 grams of the ground bacopa monnieri with 60 grams of gelatin capsules and fill 200 empty capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with dry eyes because they contain a concentrated form of marigold extract, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This natural extract helps to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and discomfort associated with dry eye syndrome. Additionally, the omega-3 fatty acids present in pot marigold oil help to moisturize and nourish the eyes, promoting healthy tear production and lubrication.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold capsules can neutralize free radicals that damage ocular tissues and contribute to dry eye symptoms, such as blurred vision and sensitivity to light.
Moisturizing agents: The mucilage content in pot marigold capsules forms a protective film on the surface of the eyes, helping to retain moisture and soothe dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenes: These compounds, particularly calendulic acid, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and irritation associated with dry eyes.

Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic acid and ferulic acid): These antioxidants can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress, which may contribute to dry eye symptoms, and also have anti-inflammatory effects.

Flavonoids (particularly, kaempferol and quercetin): Flavonoids in Calendula officinalis have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect the eyes, reducing symptoms of dry eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high content of triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for eye health.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain a range of bioactive compounds, including carotenoids and flavonoids, which help to protect and soothe dry, irritated eyes.
Roots: The roots are used due to their high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help to reduce eye inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried calendula flowers and store them in an airtight container for later use.
Use a mortar and pestle to grind the dried calendula flowers into a fine powder.
Weigh out 250mg of the ground calendula powder and mix it with 250mg of a carrier agent.
Use a capsule filler to fill 50 capsules with the calendula mixture and seal them.
Label the capsules with the date of preparation and the contents and store them in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with dry eyes because of their unique ability to nourish and soothe the eyes. The gel inside aloe vera contains powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which is often the underlying cause of dryness. Additionally, the vitamins and minerals present in aloe vera, such as vitamin A and potassium, help moisturize and hydrate the eyes, reducing dryness and irritation. This natural and gentle approach can provide long-lasting relief from dry eye symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with dry eyes by reducing inflammation in the eyes.
Moisturizing: Aloe vera's moisturizing properties help to lock in natural moisture in the eyes, relieving dryness, itchiness, and burning sensations caused by dry eye syndrome.
Antioxidant: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from free radical damage, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms such as blurred vision and fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Vitamins A and E: These antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can exacerbate dry eye symptoms.
Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin, a phenolic compound, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe and calm the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort associated with dry eyes.
Glucomannan: Glucomannan, a polysaccharide, helps maintain the integrity of the tear film, which can become compromised in dry eye conditions, leading to irritation and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves of Aloe vera is used to make capsules for dry eyes due to its soothing and moisturizing properties.
Leaves: The extract from the leaves also contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry eyes.
Leaves: The polysaccharides present in the leaves of Aloe vera help to lock in moisture and protect the eyes from further dryness and damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100-200 aloe vera leaves from mature plants at the peak of summer when the gel is most abundant.
Remove the outer skin of the leaves and extract the clear gel from the inner pulp.
Add 1 tablespoon of glycerin to 100 grams of aloe vera gel to enhance bioavailability and shelf life.
Fill gelatin capsules with 0.5 to 1 gram of the aloe vera mixture and seal the capsules tightly.
Store the capsules in a cool dark place for 24 hours before consumption to allow gel to set properly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with dry eyes because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and lubricate the eyes. The bitter compounds in dandelion, such as taraxasterol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can help alleviate symptoms of dry eye syndrome. Polysaccharides (specifically, inulin): Inulin, a type of soluble fiber, has been reported to have anti-inflammatory effects and may help maintain the health of the ocular surface, potentially alleviating dry eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Taraxacum officinale are used to make capsules for dry eyes because they are rich in flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: Roots of Taraxacum officinale are used to make capsules for dry eyes because they contain inulin, a prebiotic that can help soothe and protect the eyes.
Flowers: Flowers of Taraxacum officinale are used to make capsules for dry eyes because they are rich in carotenoids and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and promote eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of dried taraxacum officinale root in the morning when the dew is still present.
Dry the harvested taraxacum officinale root in a low temperature oven at 50°c for 24 hours.
Grind 10g of dried taraxacum officinale root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder within 5 minutes.
Mix 300mg of the taraxacum officinale powder with 600mg of carrier material in a clean container within 2 minutes.
Fill 20 empty gelatin capsules with the taraxacum officinale mixture and seal them immediately.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried aerial parts of euphrasia officinalis from a trusted supplier or cultivated area.
Weigh 1g of the dried euphrasia officinalis aerial parts using a precision balance and record the weight.
Mix the weighed euphrasia officinalis aerial parts with 2g of vegetable cellulose powder and 0.5g of silica in a bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the prepared mixture and set the machine to fill 100 capsules in about 10 minutes.
Inspect the capsules for any visible signs of contamination or debris and store them in a cool dry place.

remedy_recipe: Dry the nelumbo nucifera flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Crush the dried flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part of the powdered nelumbo nucifera with 2 parts of gelatin and 1 part of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filler machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese rhubarb capsules helps with dry eyes because they contain a potent combination of herbal ingredients that work to nourish and moisten the eyes. The key compound, Rheum palmatum, is known for its ability to increase blood flow and reduce inflammation in the delicate tissues surrounding the eyes. As a result, it can help to soothe and lubricate dry, irritated eyes, providing instant relief from discomfort and promoting healthy vision.
remedy_properties: Anthraquinone glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the eye and alleviate symptoms of dry eye syndrome.

Fiber content: The high fiber content in Chinese Rhubarb can help to promote digestive health, which is closely linked to overall eye health. Poor digestion has been linked to an increased risk of dry eye syndrome.
Sennoside A and B: These anthraquinone compounds have been shown to have anti-oxidant properties, which can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These glycoside compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate dryness in the eyes by their anti-inflammatory properties.
Flavonoids: As antioxidants, flavonoids help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, contributing to the relief of dry eye symptoms.
Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help reduce eye irritation and promote tear production, alleviating dry eye discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Rheum palmatum are primarily used for their anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, making them effective in soothing dry eyes.
Barks: The barks of Rheum palmatum contain compounds that have been traditionally used to treat dry eyes, promoting moisture and reducing irritation.
Leaves: The leaves of Rheum palmatum are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect and soothe dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Dry the rheum palmatum herb at a low temperature of 40°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture completely.
Grind the dried rheum palmatum into a fine powder using a mill or grinder to achieve uniform texture.
Mix 1 part of the rheum palmatum powder with 2 parts of gelatin or plant-based binder in a bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder-binder mixture and prepare the capsules according to the manufacturer's guidelines.
Store the herbal rheum palmatum capsules in a cool dark place for up to 6 months to maintain potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with dry eyes because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken orally, glycyrrhizin is absorbed into the bloodstream and travels to the eyes, where it reduces inflammation and irritation caused by dryness. Additionally, licorice root extract also exhibits antioxidant activity, which helps protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, herbal licorice capsules can provide relief for individuals suffering from dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and protect the eyes from oxidative stress caused by dry eye syndrome.
Flavonoids: These bioactive compounds have been shown to improve blood circulation and increase tear production, thereby helping to alleviate dry eye symptoms such as itchiness and redness.
Liquiritin: This flavonoid has been found to inhibit the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing inflammation in the eyes and promoting a healthy tear film balance.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra help with dry eyes by reducing inflammation and promoting the health of the ocular surface.

Flavonoids (including Liquiritin and Isoliquiritin): Flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra help with dry eyes by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that protect the eyes from damage caused by dryness and oxidative stress.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin in Glycyrrhiza glabra helps with dry eyes by inhibiting the production of cortisol, a hormone that can contribute to dry eye syndrome by causing inflammation and damaging the ocular surface.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to reduce dryness in the eyes.
Barks: The barks are used because they contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe and protect dry eyes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier or your own garden.
Grind the harvested roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill for 5 minutes.
Mix 20g of the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra root powder with 100g of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 200-500mg of the glycyrrhiza glabra root powder mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica capsules helps with dry eyes because they contain the herb Angelica archangelica, which has been traditionally used to soothe and moisturize the eyes. The capsules work by increasing tear production and improving the quality of tears, helping to alleviate dryness, itchiness, and irritation. Additionally, angelica's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and discomfort associated with dry eye syndrome. By promoting healthy tear function, herbal angelica capsules provide natural relief from dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Angelica capsules contain compounds like ferulic acid, which helps reduce inflammation in the eye, alleviating redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry eyes.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in angelica, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the eyes from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of eye diseases like cataracts and macular degeneration.
Vasodilator: Angelica's ability to dilate blood vessels can increase blood flow to the eyes, promoting healthy tear production and helping to moisten dry, irritated eyes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality angelica archangelica roots and dry them thoroughly in a warm dark place for 2 weeks.
Chop the dried angelica archangelica roots into small pieces and mix with 200g of organic beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and blended with the root powder.
Pour the warm mixture into empty gelatin capsules and seal them immediately to preserve freshness for up to 2 years.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place and take one capsule twice daily as needed with food and water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree peony capsules helps with dry eyes because they contain flavonoids, potent antioxidants that reduce inflammation and improve ocular health. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties soothe irritated eyes and calm the conjunctiva, alleviating symptoms of dry eye syndrome. Moisturizing: The capsules' antioxidant and humectant properties help to soothe and moisturize the eyes, reducing discomfort and improving tear film stability.

Pungency-reducing: Tree peony has been traditionally used to reduce pungent or stinging sensations in the eyes, which can occur with dry eye syndrome, by regulating the expression of genes involved in inflammation and pain signaling.

Barks: The barks of the tree peony have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm and soothe dry, itchy eyes, reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh paeonia suffruticosa roots and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the harvested roots in a clean, well-ventilated area at room temperature for 7 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or food processor.
Mix the ground root powder with 250g of cornstarch and 125ml of distilled water to form a uniform mixture that can be filled into capsules.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 300mg of the prepared mixture and seal them tightly to ensure freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horehound capsules helps with dry eyes because they contain marshmallow root, a natural demulcent that soothes and lubricates the eyes. Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory properties in horehound capsules reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with dry eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antioxidant activity: Horehound's antioxidant properties help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye syndrome and other age-related macular degeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and moisturize dry eyes.
Barks: Contain expectorant properties that help loosen and remove excess mucus from the eyes, relieving dryness and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marrubium vulgare leaves and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the cleaned leaves in a warm and ventilated area for at least 24 hours or until crisp.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for approximately 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered marrubium vulgare with 500mg of silica gel and fill empty capsules shells with 250mg of the mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein capsules helps with dry eyes because they contain a potent anti-inflammatory agent that soothes and calms irritated eye tissues. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh verbascum thapsus leaves and stems, carefully selecting only those free from contamination.
Dry the harvested material using a low-temperature dehydration method such as air circulation or desiccation for 7-10 days.
Grind dried verbascum thapsus into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, resulting in approximately 1 pound of powder.
Mix 30 grams of the powdered verbascum thapsus with 70 grams of gelatin capsules, filling 100 capsules according to manufacturer instructions within 2 hours of mixing.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal capsules helps with dry eyes because of their unique combination of natural ingredients, particularly berberine. This compound has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and irritation in the eyes. Additionally, goldenseal's antibacterial qualities help combat infections that can exacerbate dry eye symptoms. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infection, goldenseal capsules alleviate discomfort and promote healthy tear production, providing natural relief from dry, itchy, and irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness and swelling in the eyes associated with dry eye syndrome.
Berberein: Possesses antimicrobial activity, helping to combat bacterial infections that may contribute to dry eye symptoms, such as conjunctivitis or blepharitis.
Berbamin: Exhibits antioxidant properties, which can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by free radicals, a known contributor to dry eye syndrome.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in soothing dry, irritated eyes.
Barks: Containing berberine and other alkaloids, the barks of goldenseal have been traditionally used to treat eye infections and inflammation, which can contribute to dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried goldenseal root powder and gelatin capsules for filling.
Measure 250 milligrams of dried goldenseal root powder per capsule.
Fill each gelatin capsule with the measured goldenseal root powder mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature.
Consume one to two capsules orally twice daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with dry eyes because they are rich in antioxidants, particularly anthocyanins and flavonoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and discomfort caused by dry eye syndrome. Additionally, elder capsules contain omega-3 fatty acids, which support the health of the tear glands and promote the production of healthy tears, helping to keep the eyes moist and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elderberry's antioxidants help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Elderberry's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the eyes, which is a common underlying cause of dry eye syndrome and associated discomfort.
Mucilage content: The mucilages present in elderberry capsules form a soothing film on the surface of the eyes, providing lubrication and comfort to dry, irritated eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated eyes.
Barks: The barks of the elder plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers in the morning after dew has dried for maximum potency.
Dry the sambucus nigra flowers using a food dehydrator at low temperature for 8 hours.
Grind 30g of dried sambucus nigra flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 250mg of the powdered sambucus nigra and seal tightly.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry capsules helps with dry eyes because of its unique properties that address the root causes of this common issue. The herb contains powerful antioxidants and flavonoids that reduce inflammation and improve ocular blood flow, alleviating dryness and irritation in the eyes. Additionally, highbush cranberry's natural anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe and moisturize the eyes, providing long-lasting relief from dry eye symptoms such as itching, burning, and blurred vision.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to dry eye symptoms.

Vitamin A and beta-carotene: These nutrients are essential for maintaining the health of the cornea and conjunctiva, and high levels of them in highbush cranberry capsules can help support the natural moisture barrier of the eyes.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry eye syndrome by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm dry, irritated eyes.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes have been shown to possess antioxidant properties that can help protect the delicate tissues of the eye from damage caused by dryness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh vaccinium macrocarpon leaves and stems on a sunny day in late summer.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 12 hours or air drying for 3-4 days.
Grind 200g of dried vaccinium macrocarpon into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 100g of the ground powder with 50g of silica gel and store it in an airtight container for 24 hours to allow for infusing.
Fill hard shell capsules with 250mg of the infused powder mixture and seal them immediately for future use or storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose capsules helps with dry eyes because of its unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory properties, and emollients. The capsules contain extracts from the petals of the damask rose, which have been shown to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing redness and irritation. The antioxidants in the capsules also help to protect the eyes from further damage caused by free radicals, while the emollients moisturize and hydrate the eyes, providing long-lasting relief from dry, itchy, and irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Damask rose capsules contain antioxidants like vitamin C, E, and beta-carotene, which help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by dry eye syndrome.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules' anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry eyes.
Moisturizing and soothing properties: Damask rose extracts contain emollients like omega-6 fatty acids, which help lock in moisture and soothe the eyes, reducing dryness, itchiness, and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the damask rose plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Flowers: Damask rose flowers contain flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have been shown to improve blood flow to the eyes and reduce inflammation, making them effective for treating dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh rosa damascena flowers and dry them in shade or using desiccant for 24 hours.
Grind dried flowers into fine powder using coffee grinder or mortar and pestle within 3 days of drying.
Combine 100g of powdered rosa damascena with 100g of gelatin capsules and mix well for 5 minutes.
Fill capsules with airtight stainless steel scoop to ensure correct dosage of 200mg per capsule, then seal tightly.
Store prepared capsules in dark glass jar at room temperature or refrigerator for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules help with dry eyes because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation in the eyes. This inflammation is often the underlying cause of dry eye syndrome, characterized by itchiness, grittiness, and blurred vision. By reducing inflammation, turmeric capsules alleviate these symptoms, promoting healthy tear production and lubrication. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, further supporting overall ocular health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes that may contribute to dry eye symptoms.

Corticosteroid-like activity: Turmeric contains curcuminoids that have been shown to exhibit corticosteroid-like activity, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, alleviating dryness and discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of turmeric's polyphenols can help neutralize free radicals that may contribute to oxidative stress and damage to the cornea and conjunctiva, leading to dry eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in soothing and reducing inflammation associated with dry eyes.
Buds: Containing high amounts of volatile oils, these buds help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the ocular surface, relieving dry eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality curcuma longa rhizomes and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the cleaned rhizomes in a well-ventilated area for at least seven days.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with 100 grams of gelatin and 50 milliliters of distilled water to form a uniform mixture.
Encapsulate the mixture in size zero capsules and store them in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea capsules helps with dry eyes because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds found in certain herbs, which help to soothe and calm irritated eyes. These herbal extracts, such as eyebright and chamomile, can increase tear production and reduce inflammation, providing relief from dry, itchy, and gritty eyes. Additionally, the omega-3 fatty acids present in some herbal tea capsules may also help to nourish and lubricate the eyes, further alleviating symptoms of dry eye syndrome.
remedy_properties: Quercetin: A flavonoid antioxidant that reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms such as redness and irritation.
Licorice root extract: Contains compounds like glycyrrhizin that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the eyes, reducing dryness and discomfort.

Marshmallow root: Rich in mucilages, which are natural emollients that help lubricate and soothe the eyes, providing relief from dryness, itchiness, and burning sensations associated with dry eye syndrome.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in soothing dry, irritated eyes.
Barks: Contains flavonoids and tannins that help to reduce inflammation and improve eye health.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality green tea leaves of camellia sinensis and dried licorice root at dosages of 200mg and 100mg respectively.
Dry the green tea leaves in a warm oven at 150°f for two hours to preserve their bioactive compounds.
Grind the dried green tea leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered green tea with the dried licorice root in a small bowl at a ratio of 2:1 by weight and fill gelatin capsules.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months before consumption.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for dry eyes are a natural supplement designed to provide relief from the symptoms of dry, irritated, or inflamed eyes. These capsules typically contain a blend of herbal extracts that help to soothe and moisturize the eyes, reducing redness, itchiness, and discomfort. Examples include capsicum, chamomile, and eyebright, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. By using these herbal capsules, individuals with dry eyes can experience improved vision, reduced eye fatigue, and increased comfort while reading, working on computers, or engaging in other activities that normally exacerbate dry eyes.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry eyes that are treated with herbal capsules are conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and meibomian gland dysfunction. These conditions often cause inflammation, redness, and irritation of the eyes. Herbal capsules containing ingredients such as bilberry, eyebright, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the affected area, reducing discomfort and promoting healthy vision.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with dry eyes is a blend of Eyebright, Goldenseal, and N-Acetyl Cysteine (NAC). Eyebright is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling. Goldenseal has antimicrobial properties that combat infections, while NAC is an antioxidant that helps to protect the eyes from damage. This combination can help to soothe and heal dry, irritated eyes by reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting overall eye health. This blend may also improve tear quality and reduce symptoms of dry eyes.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, Buddleja officinalis Maxim. (B. officinalis), a traditional herbal medicine, has been used for thousands of years to treat dry eye disease. The study, which collected information from various electronic resources including the Web of Science, PubMed, and Google Scholar, found that extracts and compounds derived from B. officinalis possess broad pharmacological effects including anti-dry eye disease, anti-inflammation, and anti-oxidation. These properties suggest that herbal capsules containing B. officinalis may be beneficial for individuals suffering from dry eyes by reducing inflammation and promoting eye health.
